rulings.Case Title: ANNA ESTRADA VS. SALLY LIU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 17, 2021 line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT EDWARD HIGGINBOTHAM'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Claims based on the following allegations are stricken "cross-defendants filed a groundless lawsuit.. orally uttered false allegations concerning the content of the complaint filed an opposition.. to demurrer and requested sanctions," including claims for abuse of process, malicious prosecution, conspiracy, intentional infliction of emotional distress, UCL, 8-HUD Fraud arising out of the above-quoted allegations. Cross-Complaint failed to establish probability of prevailing on the merits with competent admissible evidence; also CC 47(b). Motion is denied as to the claim for fraud, trespass, violation of rent ordinance (nuisance) health code, quiet title and partition. Moving party failed to sustain its burden and identify claims arising out of protected activity. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANNA ESTRADA VS. SALLY LIU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Second Motion To Strike All Causes Of Actions As To Defendant Liu'S Cross-Complaint Pursuant To Cccp 415.16
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 17, 2021 line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT ANNA ESTRADA, TIMOTHY WORKMAN Notice Of Motion And Second Motion To Strike All Causes Of Actions As To Defendant Liu'S Cross-Complaint Pursuant To Cccp 415.16 is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Claims based on the following allegations are stricken "cross-defendants filed a groundless lawsuit.. orally uttered false allegations concerning the content of the complaint filed an opposition.. to demurrer and requested sanctions" and paragraphs 29-31 including claims for abuse of process, malicious prosecution, conspiracy, intentional infliction of emotional distress, UCL and 8-HUD Fraud arising out of the above-quoted allegations. Cross-Complaint failed to establish probability of prevailing on the merits with competent admissible evidence; also CC 47(b). Motion is denied as to the claims for fraud, trespass, violation of rent ordinance (nuisance) health code, quiet title and partition. Moving party failed to sustain its burden and identify claims arising out of protected activity as to these matters. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 152 GEARY ST. LLC, VS. VIJAYA PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 17, 2021 line 3. PLAINTIFF 152 GEARY ST. LLC, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party moved for a summary judgment claiming entitlement to a judgment of $8,237,572.96 against Vijaya Properties LLC and Interurban Capital Group, Inc. In reply, Plaintiff attempts to sidestep the issue of damages by seeking only $6,961,657.83 judgement, plus daily damages (Note: the relevant portions of the reply brief are devoid of any authority Reply 6:10-21). The motions are framed by the Notice of Motion. Here, Plaintiff appears to have abandoned its original request as framed by the Notice in an effort to avoid a triable issue of fact. (Reply 2:3-6). Motion for summary judgment was filed on August 9, 2021, 9 months after $1,050,000 was applied to unpaid rent under the Lease. (Levin Declaration, paragraph 8). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: 2000 FOLSOM PARTNERS LLC VS. EAT JUST, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 17, 2021 line 9. DEFENDANT EAT JUST, INC. DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. On a demurrer, the Court must treat Plaintiff's interpretation of the contractual provisions as true. Plaintiff alleges that in accordance with the contractual provisions September 2, 2021 notice was served on the Facilities Director and provided 5 days to cure. Plaintiff further alleges that upon failure to cure under September 2, 2021, defendant was served in accordance with the law with a 3 Day Notice to Pay or Quit. This service was accomplished by substitute service on the Tenant by leaving a copy with the Front Desk Attendant. These allegations are sufficient to state a cause of action for unlawful detainer under the Lease and the relevant statutes. There is no other action pending between the parties for recovery of possession. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
